rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for bindings after subcomponents and connections.
Use visible feature names and concrete bounds when defining range assumptions and guarantees in component type contracts.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE annex content aligns with AADL component scope rules and preserves architectural integrity.
Use boolean expressions to define value range conditions in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Declare helper variables with explicit types matching the expression result (e.g., bool for comparisons).
Place annex blocks in valid AADL scopes: component types for contracts, implementations for bindings.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or using subcomponent ports in type contracts.
Do not define range assumptions without visible feature names or concrete bounds.
